avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Very poor stability overnight on all North through East to South East aspects above 900m; avalanches likely. All new and recently formed cornices will become weak and prone to sudden collapse. Good stability on all ridges and recently wind scoured aspects. After overnight instability all snow will be wet but slowly recover and exhibit some early consolidation by midday.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Heavy rainfall overnight easing to showers during the day. Very mild: freezing level rising to over 2000m overnight, becoming 1700m later. Very strong South South Westerly winds.
Observed Weather Influences: Cold overnight with frequent occasionally heavy snow showers, easing a little during the day. Strong and turbulent West South Westerly airflow throughout. Some warmth from the sun during occasional cloud breaks. Noticeably milder from early afternoon onwards. No rainfall at the time of reporting.
ObservedSnowStability: Continuous drifting led to deep and widespread weak windslab development on most North through East to East South East aspects above 900m. Noticeably better stability at depth in all snow at lower altitudes. Good stability on all recently wind exposed locations. Widespread minor surface point releases and roller ball activity below 850m on recent lee slopes. The area was unaffected by rainfall at the time of reporting.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The significant deposits of unstable windslab which exists on North to East aspects above 1000 metres will become wet throughout during the period. Avalanches will be likely in these locations and cornices above these aspects will be prone to collapse. However, windward aspects will tend to be scoured and stability will be better. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be mild with the freezing level remaining above the summits. There will be rain though out the period, heavy at times. Winds will strong Southerly
Observed Weather Influences: Snow showers overnight and during the morning gradually turned to rain through the afternoon. The freezing rose from around 600 metres early morning to summit level by late afternoon. Winds were from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: Significant quantities of unstable windslab have been deposited in wind-sheltered locations on mainly North to East aspects above 900 metres. This was becoming progressively more unstable as the freezing level rose and the snowpack started to became wet. Some large cornices exist above these aspects, and these are prone to collapse.  The avalanche hazard is High

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Wet snow instabilities will develop into the evening and overnight where deeper accumulations are present on steep slopes. Terrain features such as gullies and coire rims on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres will be most affected. Cornices will be prone to collapse. Some consolidation will occur as precipitation eases during the morning. Elsewhere the snowpack will continue to thaw and be generally well bonded. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A warm front will spread eastwards bringing heavy rain and drizzle. The heaviest rain will be during the evening and overnight, then clearing in the early hours to leave some light and patchy rain before further heavy showers during the afternoon. The freezing level will be above the summits. Gale to storm force South-South-Westerly winds.
Observed Weather Influences: Heavy snow showers fell overnight to around 400 metres. Dryer for a time during the morning before further precipitation gradually turned to rain at higher elevations.  The freezing level was 600 meters then rose to around the summits during the day. Gale force South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised areas of moderately bonded windslab are present in some steep wind sheltered locations. North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres are most affected. Elsewhere there is a thin covering of stable snow. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Windslab will become moist with the onset of rain resulting in a transition from dry to wet snow instability. Areas affected will be North through North-East to South-East aspects above 950 metres. Steep slopes such as coire headwalls between 700 and 950 metres will also be affected. Cornices will be prone to collapse, triggering steep slopes below. Elsewhere, the snowpack will be moist and subject to slow consolidation. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Rain at all elevations is expected overnight and through much of the day. Winds will generally be from the South-South-West remaining storm force for the duration. The freezing level will remain well above the highest summits.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight snow above 600 metres preceded snow showers during the day confined to above 800 metres. Late in the period precipitation fell as rain with sleet at summit levels. Winds were gale force and from the West-South-West. The freezing level was around 850 metres at midday, rising above the summits later.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab is present in steep wind sheltered areas above 950 metres. Gully tops and coire rims of a North-East to East aspect are mostly affected with cross loaded deposits elsewhere. Areas exposed to the wind are scoured or present a very firm and icy snow surface. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: A period of higher instability will occur as heavy rain affects all levels overnight. Wet snow instabilities will develop on steep slopes where accumulations of windslab exist. Most affected will be sheltered gullies and slopes with a North-West through North to South-Easterly aspect mainly above 700 metres, Avalanches and cornice collapse are likely. Stability will start to improve with a return to drier conditions.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Heavy rain and drizzle will affect all levels overnight. The heaviest rain clearing through the early hours of Tuesday leaving some light and patchy rain and drizzle. Further heavy rain will affect all levels later in the day. Gale force winds will be generally Southerly.
Observed Weather Influences: New snow and strong generally West-South-Westerly winds overnight and today have affected the area. The freezing level is around 850 metres and rising. Much milder conditions will occur later in the day.
ObservedSnowStability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow overnight and earlier today have formed accumulations of deep unstable windslab in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a North-West through North to South-Easterly aspect mainly above 800 metres. Fragile cornices exist above these locations. Wind exposed slopes have been scoured and are firm and icy in places.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Wet snow instabilities will become more widespread from early evening onwards. These instabilities will be found mainly in the pre-existing windslab on North-West through North-East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops, coire rims and steep wind sheltered locations most affected. Unstable cornices will be prone to collapse on these same aspects. The existing old snowpack will slowly soften but will have good stability. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will remain well above summit levels for the duration of the forecast period. A wet evening following by some interrupted drizzle afterwards. Strong South-South-Westerlies will increase to gale force by dawn.
Observed Weather Influences: Light overnight snow to low levels cleared to leave a mainly dry morning with rain moving in during the afternoon. Gale force Westerlies eased through the day backing South-West by midday. The freezing level rose through the day, around 950 metres at noon.
ObservedSnowStability: Moderately bonded windslab is present in wind sheltered locations on North-West through North-East to South-East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops, sharply defined coire rims and steep wind sheltered locations are most affected. Small fragile cornices are present on these same aspects. The old snowpack is well bonded, icy and firm in nature. At lower elevations, the snowpack slowly softened during the day. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
